question: Experiments on song development in birds have shown that when a young male reared in isolation hears only the song of a different bird species, he will develop an adult song repertoire that lacks certain characteristics typical of his own species. This result shows that the song of his species is most likely
options:
A: entirely learned during development 
B: entirely instinctive 
C: both instinctive and learned 
D: dependent upon hormones for proper development
llm_explanation:
This question requires an understanding of the concept of instinctive and learned behaviors in animals. The correct answer, C, suggests that the song of a bird species is both instinctive and learned. This is because the young male bird develops an adult song repertoire that lacks certain characteristics typical of his own species when he is reared in isolation and hears only the song of a different bird species. This implies that the young bird has an innate predisposition to learn and mimic certain aspects of song, but the specifics of the song are learned through interaction with other birds.

statement: The song of a bird species is both instinctive and learned.

question: Experiments on song development in birds have shown that when a young male reared in isolation hears only the song of a different bird species, he will develop an adult song repertoire that lacks certain characteristics typical of his own species. This result shows that the song of his species is most likely
options:
A: entirely learned during development 
B: entirely instinctive 
C: both instinctive and learned 
D: dependent upon hormones for proper development
llm_explanation: The experiment demonstrates that while the young male bird can learn songs, it cannot develop the full repertoire characteristic of its own species without exposure to its own species' song. This suggests that the innate aspects of the song (instinctive) are crucial, alongside learned elements. Therefore, the correct answer is C: both instinctive and learned.
